name: Pressure Cooker Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: 4-6 Potatoes, peeled and sliced, somewhat thick slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat your pressure cooker on medium-high heat and pour enough oil to coat the bottom.
2. Once hot, brown the pork chops until they are dark but not burned. This renders better gravy and makes them tastier.
3. Brown all sides of the pork chops as much as possible. Remove the chops to a plate.
4. Reduce the heat to medium. Add the sliced potatoes (watch out for hot grease) and water. Stir to coat the potatoes thoroughly.
5. Add the chopped onion (do not stir). Place the chops on top of the potatoes.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper if desired.
7. Put the lid back on and set a timer for 20 minutes once the pressure cooker's whistle rocks.
8. Cool the cooker by running water over the top.
9. Remove the chops to your serving plate along with the potatoes. The chops will be very tender, and the potatoes will be soaked in gravy.
10. Serves 4. This is not a fast recipe because it takes time for enough pressure to build at medium heat for the whistle to rock. Increasing the heat may cause burning of the potatoes. My mother used to make this recipe with good results.
